name: Day 1 - Back
description: My back day in Hevy. Two progressive warm-ups (W1/W2) then all-out failure sets (F1/F2).

exercises.notes: Chest up, elbows to hips. 1-2s stretch pause on the last rep.
Full stretch, slight torso twist.
Pure hinge, neutral spine. 10s iso-hold at the end. First set easy.

name: Day 2 - Chest + Biceps
description: My chest and biceps day in Hevy.

name: Day 4 - Legs (Quad-priority)
description: My quad-priority leg day in Hevy.

exercises.notes: Lock hips down, control the swing. 10-15s iso-hold + 2-3 partials on the last rep. First set very light.
2-3s stretch, 1s peak hold. Drop ~25% then rep to a burn. First set easy with long stretches.

name: Day 4 - Legs (Ham-priority)
description: My hamstring-priority leg day variant in Hevy.

exercises.notes: Hinge, 2s stretch, keep the bar close to the shins.
Depth with pelvis control, push through the mid-foot. 3-4 slow eccentrics at the end.
Lock hips down, control the swing. 10-15s iso-hold + 2-3 partials on the last rep.
Posterior pelvic tilt, no neck pull. Full exhale at the top.

name: Day 5 - Shoulders + Triceps
description: My shoulders and triceps day in Hevy.

exercises.notes: Sweep wide, avoid the traps. First set very light.
Elbows tucked, press through the heel of the palm.
Split the rope at the bottom, no shoulder drift. 10s stretch on the last rep. First set very light.

name: Walking = Good
description: Low-intensity steady-state cardio for off-season conditioning.

exercises.notes: Fat-burning HR = (220 - age) x 50-70%. e.g. 30y ~122-144 bpm, 40y ~115-137 bpm.

name: Classic - Chest & Biceps
description: Reference Blood & Guts chest and biceps session as Dorian Yates ran it: a couple of warm-ups then one all-out working set (F1) taken beyond failure.

exercises.notes: Two warm-ups then one working set to failure + 2-3 forced reps.

name: Classic - Back & Rear Delts
description: Reference Blood & Guts back session. Yates pre-exhausted the lats with pullovers before heavy rows and finished with deadlifts.

exercises.notes: Lat pre-exhaust.
The signature Yates underhand row. Straps on, one all-out set.

name: Ideal Routine - Workout 1: Chest & Back
description: Mentzer's Heavy Duty Ideal Routine, Workout 1. Do 1-3 warm-ups per exercise, then ONE all-out working set to failure. Rest 4-7+ days before the next workout in the rotation.

exercises.notes: Pre-exhaust. Straight into the incline press with no rest.
Post-exhaust press to failure immediately after the pec deck.
Close-grip, palms-up pulldown. One set to failure.

setScheme.label: F1
setScheme.intensity: One all-out set to momentary muscular failure

exercises.notes: One heavy set to near-failure. Shrugs can be substituted if needed.

setScheme.intensity: One all-out set to momentary muscular failure

name: Ideal Routine - Workout 2: Legs
description: Mentzer's Heavy Duty Ideal Routine, Workout 2. Pre-exhaust the quads with extensions straight into the leg press. One working set each to failure.

exercises.notes: Pre-exhaust, straight into the leg press.
Squat may be substituted if no leg press.

exercises.notes: Heavy static hold to failure (10-25 seconds), then straight into squats.

name: Consolidation Routine - Workout A
description: The most minimal evolution of Heavy Duty (Heavy Duty II). Two compound lifts, one working set each, performed once every 5-7+ days. Alternate Workout A and B.

exercises.notes: One all-out set to failure after warm-ups.

name: HTLT Hardcore - Legs
description: Representative HTLT Hardcore leg day following Doucette's public principles: controlled 2-1-1 tempo, target the rep range, warm-ups stop 3-5 reps shy of failure, and only the last set of each movement goes 'all out'. Beat last time.

exercises.notes: Controlled tempo, 3s+ per rep. Last set all out.

name: HTLT Hardcore - Back & Shoulders

exercises.notes: Add weight or use a band to stay in range.
